Farn Pourtcal PnacrICES CouvtIssIoN
428 J Street . Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
July 11,2006
Ray Luna, Vice-Mayor
City of Santa Paula
634 gth Street
Santa Paula, California 93060
RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-06-112
Dear Mr. Luna:
This letter is in response to your request regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r Please note that nothing in this letter
should be construed to evaluate any conduct which may have already taken place. The
Comrnission will decline to provide advice related to conduct that has already occurred.
(Regulation 18329.) In addition, this letter is based on the facts presented to us. The
Comrnission does not act as the finder of fact in providing advice. Qn re Oglesby (1975)
1 FPPC Ops. 71.) Because you do not provide sufficient facts regarding the specific
decisions related to the project, we are treating your request as one for informal
assistance. (Regulation 1 8329(b)(8)).2
QUESTION
May you participate in decisions concerning the development of an apartment
project to be built on a lot of land which includes within its boundaries, an existing
building from which you conduct areal estate business?
CONCLUSION
You will have a conflict of interest only if it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will have amateial financial effect on your economic interests. See discussion
below.
' Go,rernm"nt Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
' Pl"ase note that informal assistance does not confer the immunity provided by a Commission
opinion or formal written advice. (Regulation 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)
r*'-ii,,i


File No. I-06-112
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are areal estate agent employed with Ray Balderas Realty within the City of
Santa Paula. Ray Balderas Realty is located in a buildingonT18 E. Main Street in Santa
Paula.
The city council is considering a proposal to build an eighrunit apartment
complex on a lot of land which includes within its boundaries the building from which
Ray Balderas Realty conducts business.3 During a telephone call on Juni 74,2006, you
stated that the land is owned by a third party, who rents in a month-to-month agreement
office space to Ray Balderas Realty. You also stated that the project would not have any
impact on the amount of rent Ray Balderas Realty pays or the location of its office.
On June 5,2006, the city council had a tie vote regarding the proposed project.
You were absent from that meeting, and you had earlier declared a conflict in the
decision due to advice from the city attorney.
During a phone call on June 14,2006, you stated that a final decision on the
project will be considered by the city council sometime in the near future. You wish to
seek formal advice from the Commission to determine if you actually have a conflict in
this decision. However, you were not able to provide any facts relating to the financial
effects of the project on Ray Balderas Realty.
ANALYSIS
Conflict-of-Interest Prohibition
Your question implicates the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions which ensure
that pubiic officials "perform their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused
by their own financial interests or the financial interests of persons who have supported
them." (Section 81001(b).) Specifically, section 87l00prohibits anypublic official from
making, participating in making, or otherwise using his or her official position to
influence a goverrmental decision in which the official has a financial interest.
A public official has a "financial interest" irt a govemmental decision within the
meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the governmental decision will
have a material financial effect on one or more of the public official's economic interests.
(Section 87103; regulation 1 8700(a).) The Commission has adopted a standard eight-step
analysis for deciding whether an individual has a disqualiflring conflict of interest in a
given govemmental decision. (Regulation 1 8 700(b) ( 1 )-(8). )
3 The proposal contemplates the construction of the apartments behind the building housing Ray
Balderas Realty


File No. I-06-lt2
Page No. 3
1. Are you a ,.public officialrr?
The conflict-of-interest prohibition only applies to public officials. (Section
87100.) As a council member in the City of SantaPaula, you are a "member, officer,
employee or consultant of a state or local government agency" and are,therefore, a public
official subject to the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act. (Section g204g;
regulation 18701(a).)
2- will you be making, participating in making, or influencing a
governmental decision?
The confl ict-of-interest prohibition covers specifi c conduct: making, participating
in making, or attempting to use one's official position to influence a goverimental
decision. (Section 87100.) The Commission has adopted a series ofregulations which
define "making," "pafiicipating in making," and "influen cing,, u gor"rirr"ntal decision,
and which provide certain exceptions. (Regulati ons lg7o2-1g70,.4.)
A public official "makes a governmental decision" when the official, acting
within the authority of his or her office or position, votes on a matter, obligates or
cornmits his or her agency to any course of action, determines not to act because of a
conflict, or enters into any contractual agreement on behalf of his or her agency.
(Regulation 18702.1.)
A public official "participates in making" a govemmental decision when he or
she, without significant substantive review, negotiates, advises, or makes
recommendations regarding a decision. (Regulation I S7 02.2.)
A public official is attempting to use his or her official position to ,.influence,, a
decision if for the purpose of influencing the decision, the official contacts, or appears
before any member, officer, employee, or consultant of his or her agency. (Regulation
18702.3.)
By deliberating, voting, committing your agency to a course of action, entering
into any contractual agreement on behalf of the city council with regard to the proposed
apartment development, you would be engaging in activity regulated by the Act. if you
negotiate, advise or make recommendations without significant substantive review with
regard to this project, you would also be participating in making a governmental decision.
3. What are your economic interests - 
the possible sources of a conflict of
interest?
Section 87103 provides that apublic official has a "financial interest,, in a
governmental decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
mateial financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the


File No. I-06-112
Page No. 4
official, a member of his or her immediate famlly, or on any of the official's economic
interests, described as follows:
o An economic interest in a business entity in which he or she has a direct or
indirect investment of $2,000 ormore (section 87103(a);regulation 18703.1(a)); or in
which he or she is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds anyposition
of management (section 8 7 1 03 (d); regulation I 87 03.1 (b));
o An economic interest in real property in which he or she has a direct or indirect
interest of $2,000 or more (section 87103(b); regulation 15703.2);
o An economic interest in any source of income,a including commission income,s
which aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months prior to the decision (section
87 1 03(c); regulation 187 03.3);
o An economic interest in any source of gifts to hirn or her if the gifts aggregate to
$360 or more within 12 months prior to the decision (section 871031e); r"grtutio,
18703.4).
In addition, a public official always has an economic inte.rest in his or her
personal finances, including those of his or her immediate family -- this is the..personal
financial effects" rule (section 87103; regulation 18703.5).
Economic interests disclosed in vour request for assistance
Rav Balderas Realtv: You have an economic interest in Ray Balderas Realty as a
source of commission income to you under regulation 18703.3(a)(1) if you receive
commission income from the company of $500 or more in the l2-months prior to the
decision.6 You also have an economic interest in the business under section 87103(d)
because you are an employee or position of management within the company.
(Regulation I 8703. 1 (b) )
4 "Income" is defined to include any cornmunity properly interest in the income of a spouse and a
pto rata share of the income of any business entity or kust in which the official (or his or her spouse) owns
directly, indirectly, or beneficially, a 10-percent or greater interest. (Section 82030(a).)
' "Commission income" is defined as the "gross payments received by a public official as a result
of services rendered as a broker, agent, or other salesperson for a specific sale or similar transaction."
(Regulation I 8703.3(c)(l ).)
o Your letter states that you are a real estate agent. (Section 87103(a).) You do not provide
additional details therefore we are unable to determine whether you may have other business-interests that
may be affected' Please note, however, that'possession ofa real estate sales or brokerage license, or any
other professional license, without regard to the official's business activity or likely business activity, dols
not in itself make a material financial effect on the official's economic interest reasonably foreseeable.,,
(Regulation I 8706(c).)


File No. I-06-112
Page No. 5
You have not identified other economic interests such as other sources of
incomeT, real property or other businesses you may own, therefore we do not include
them in our analysis, but point out that you must still determine whether a conflict of
interest will arise from any of them (i.e., whether it is reasonably foreseeable that the
governmental decision you participate in will result in a materi al financial effect on any
of these economic interests).
Clients: You have also not identified particular clients of yours who qualify as
sources of income,8 or who have provided you with income of $500 or more *ithi, tt "
pior l2-month period leading to the governmental decision at issue here. We therefore
do not include them further in our analysis, but point out that you must still determine
whether any of your clients qualifu as a source of incorne and whether a conflict of
interest will arise from any of them (i.e., whether it is reasonably foreseeable that the
goverlmental decision you participate in will result in a material financial effect on any
of these clients who are economic interests of yours).
Personal Ftnances.' You also have an economic interest in your personal finances
and those of your immediate family. (Regulation 18703.5.) A governrnental decision
will have an effect on this economic interest if the decision will result in the personal
expenses, income, assets or liabilities of the official or his or her immediate family
increasing or decreasing. (fbid.)
Your letter does not mentiofi any impact on your personal finances as the result of
the upcoming decision, but note that a conflict of interest may arise if it reasonably
foreseeable that the decision to approve or deny the apartrnent project will have an effect
on your personal finances.
4. Are your economic interests directly or indirectly involved in the
governmental decision ?
Ray Balderas Realt!: A person, including business entities, sources of income,
and sources of gifts, is directly involved in a decision before an official's agency when
that person, either directly or by an agent:
7 Income of an individual also includes a pro ruta share of any income of any business entity or
trust in which the individual or spouse owns, directly, indirectly or beneficially a 10 percent interest or
greater. (This also includes the official's community property interest in his or her spouse's income.)
(Section 82030.)
8 In section 87103(c), the term "source of income" includes not only the source of income which
has been received by the public official, but also the source of income which has been promised. Income is
"promised" within the meaning of our regulations when the public official has a present, legally
enforceable right to receive the promised income. (Regulation 18703.3(a); See also Hoch idv]ce Letter,
No. 4-02-060).


File No. I-06-112
Page No. 6
"(1) lnitiates the proceeding in which the decision will be made by
filing an application, claim, appeal, or similar request or; e) Is a named
party in, or is the subject of the proceeding concerning the decision before
the official or the official's a9ency. A person is the subject of a
proceeding if a decision involves the issuance, renewal, approval, denial
or revocation of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or contract
with, the subject person." (Regulation 18704.1(a).)
Your facts do not suggest that Ray Balderas Realty, your source of commission
income, is a named party in, or the subject of the proceedings concerning the proposal to
build the S-unit apartment complex. Ray Balderas Realty has a month-to-month rental
agreement and does not own the property which is the subject of the decision- The
property is owned by a third-party that is separate from the realty business. You also
stated that the project would not affect the rent or location of realty business. Therefore,
we assume the proceeding does not involve a license, permit or other entitlement to, or
contract with, the realty business. If this is the case, your source of income is indirectly
involved in this decision.e
5. Applicable Materiality Standard
A conflict of interest may arise only when the reasonably foreseeable impact of a
governmental decision on a public official's economic interests is material. (Regulation
18700(a).) Different standards apply to determine whether reasonably foreseeable
financial effect on an economic interest will be material, depending on the nature of the
economic interest and whether that interest is directly or indirectly involved in the
agency's decision.
Ray Balderas Realt-v:
The financial effect of a governmental decision on business entities vary based on
the financial size of the business. For example, the financial effect of a decision on a
small business entity, which is not directly involved in the decision, is rnaterial if it is
reasonably foreseeable that :
"(A) The governmental decision will result in an increase or
decrease in the business entity's gross revenues for a fiscal year in the
amount of $20,000 or more; or,
"(B) The goverTrmental decision will result in the business entity
incurring or avoiding additional expenses or reducing or eliminating
existing expenses for a fiscal year in the amount of $5,000 or more; or,
e You have not provided specific details regarding the nature of the upcoming decision. please
note that if the proceeding involves a license, permit or other entitlement to or contract with Rav Balderas
Realty then your source of income would be considered directly involved in the decision.


File No. I-06-112
Page No. 7
"(C) The govefilmental decision will result in an increase or
decrease in the value of the business entity's assets or liabilities of
$20,000 or more." (Regulation 18705.1(cX4).)
Personal Finances.' A reasonably foreseeable financial effect on a public
official's personal finances is material if it is at least $250 in any 72-month period.
However, when determining whether a governrnental decision has a financial
effect on a public official's economic interest in his or her personal finances, neither a
ftnancial effect on the value of real property owned directly or indirectly by the official,
nor a financial effect on the gross revenues, expenses, or value of assets and liabilities of
a business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment interest shall be
considered. (Regulation 18705.5(a).)
6. Foreseeabilify
Whether the financial consequences of a decision are reasonably foreseeable at
the time a goverlmental decision is made depends on the facts of each particular case.
As used here, "reasonably foreseeable" means "substantially likely." (Regulation
18706; In re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 198.) Certainty is not required; a substantial
likelihood that a financial effect will occur suffices to meet the standard. On the other
hand, if an effect is only a mere possibility, it is not reasonably foreseeable. (Ibid.)
Rav Balderas Realt-v: You have not provided any facts regarding potential
financial impacts on Ray Balderas Realty.
The Commission does not act as a finder of fact when providing assistance; this
assistance is based solely on the facts you provide. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FppC Ops.
71.) Therefore, the determination of whether or not it is reasonably foreseeable that ihe
applicable materiality standard will be met for your source of commission income as the
result of decisions involving the development of the 8-unit apartment complex, is
necessarily a factual question that is ultimately for you to decide.
Because the Commission is not a finder of fact, you must make a good faith effort
to assess whether your economic interests will experience a financial effect which rises to
the monetary thresholds provided above.
A good faith effort to assess the effect of the decision includes use of some
reasonable and objective method of valuation. (lrloockAdvice Letter, No. A-01-150.) If
you determine that your economic interest will be mateially affected as a result of the
decision involving development of the apartment complex, you will have a conflict of
interest in that decision.


File No. I-06-112
Page No. 8
Personal Finances.' You have not provided any informationregarding potential
irnpact on yourpersonal finances; therefore we are unable to reach a determination as to
this issue.
7. & 8. The o'Public Generally" and ,oLegally Required participation,,
Exceptions
An official who otherwise has a conflict of interest in a decision may still
participate under the "public generally" exception. This exception applies when the
frnancial effect of a decision on a public official's economic interests is substantially the
same as the effect on a significant segment of the public.
The "legally required participation" rule applies when the official's participation
in a governmental decision is legally required. (Section 871O1; regulation 18708.)
You have not presented any facts indicating that these exceptions are applicable to
your situation, therefore we do not address them here.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter,please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosure

However, when determining whether a governrnental decision has a financial
effect on a public official's economic interest in his or her personal finances, neither a
ftnancial effect on the value of real property owned directly or indirectly by the official,
nor a financial effect on the gross revenues, expenses, or value of assets and liabilities of
a business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment interest shall be
considered. (Regulation 18705.5(a).)
6. Foreseeabilify
Whether the financial consequences of a decision are reasonably foreseeable at
the time a goverlmental decision is made depends on the facts of each particular case.
As used here, "reasonably foreseeable" means "substantially likely." (Regulation
18706; In re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 198.) Certainty is not required; a substantial
likelihood that a financial effect will occur suffices to meet the standard. On the other
hand, if an effect is only a mere possibility, it is not reasonably foreseeable. (Ibid.)
Rav Balderas Realt-v: You have not provided any facts regarding potential
financial impacts on Ray Balderas Realty.
The Commission does not act as a finder of fact when providing assistance; this
assistance is based solely on the facts you provide. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FppC Ops.
71.) Therefore, the determination of whether or not it is reasonably foreseeable that ihe
applicable materiality standard will be met for your source of commission income as the
result of decisions involving the development of the 8-unit apartment complex, is
necessarily a factual question that is ultimately for you to decide.
Because the Commission is not a finder of fact, you must make a good faith effort
to assess whether your economic interests will experience a financial effect which rises to
the monetary thresholds provided above.
A good faith effort to assess the effect of the decision includes use of some
reasonable and objective method of valuation. (lrloockAdvice Letter, No. A-01-150.) If
you determine that your economic interest will be mateially affected as a result of the
decision involving development of the apartment complex, you will have a conflict of
interest in that decision.
Personal Finances.' You have not provided any informationregarding potential
irnpact on yourpersonal finances; therefore we are unable to reach a determination as to
this issue.
7. & 8. The o'Public Generally" and ,oLegally Required participation,,
Exceptions
An official who otherwise has a conflict of interest in a decision may still
participate under the "public generally" exception. This exception applies when the
frnancial effect of a decision on a public official's economic interests is substantially the
same as the effect on a significant segment of the public.
The "legally required participation" rule applies when the official's participation
in a governmental decision is legally required. (Section 871O1; regulation 18708.)
You have not presented any facts indicating that these exceptions are applicable to
your situation, therefore we do not address them here.

The Comrnission will decline to provide advice related to conduct that has already occurred. (Regulation 18329.) In addition, this letter is based on the facts presented to us. (Regulation 1 8329(b)(8)).2 QUESTION May you participate in decisions concerning the development of an apartment project to be built on a lot of land which includes within its boundaries, an existing building from which you conduct areal estate business? Ray Balderas Realty is located in a buildingonT18 E. Main Street in Santa Paula. The city council is considering a proposal to build an eighrunit apartment complex on a lot of land which includes within its boundaries the building from which Ray Balderas Realty conducts business.3 During a telephone call on Juni 74,2006, you stated that the land is owned by a third party, who rents in a month-to-month agreement office space to Ray Balderas Realty. On June 5,2006, the city council had a tie vote regarding the proposed project. You were absent from that meeting, and you had earlier declared a conflict in the decision due to advice from the city attorney. During a phone call on June 14,2006, you stated that a final decision on the project will be considered by the city council sometime in the near future.
